metadata_json.notes: Model-judged grading using RF_note.md preview and deterministic artifact-contract precheck. All rubric items passed based on the provided note and artifact presence.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include explicit unit labels in the spreadsheet cells (e.g., 'Mbps' and '%') and ensure the .xlsx formulas are accessible; a short screenshot or CSV export could help reproducibility given the environment's inability to parse the workbook directly.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All four per-cell raw inputs are present in RF_note.md (PRB util, SINR, users, throughput). Throughput/user values are computed correctly (A: 610/220=2.7727, B: 480/310=1.5484, C: 530/140=3.7857) and documented. Validation notes explicitly state required inputs are present and units are as expected.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Explicitly show the boolean comparison expressions in the spreadsheet (e.g., formulas cells like =PRB>0.85) to remove any ambiguity for automated parsing.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Decision rules were applied exactly as specified: PRB>85% (A:false, B:true, C:false), SINR<12 dB (A:false, B:true, C:false), throughput/user<2 Mbps (A:false, B:true, C:false). The RF_note documents the threshold operators and shows the numeric comparisons.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Document the exact numeric scoring or tie-breaker rule (e.g., +1 per rule hit or weighted scoring) in the spreadsheet to make the mapping explicitly reproducible.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Prioritization maps rule hits to priorities: Cell B triggers all three rules and is prioritized P1; A has no rule hits and is P3 (watch), C has headroom and is P4. The note documents the mapping and justifies ranking based on number and severity of rule breaches.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: For the high-priority actions, include expected indicator thresholds for follow-up checks (e.g., PRB busy-hour >85% over X days, SINR distribution percentiles) and any safe rollback/mitigation steps if field changes could be harmful.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: RF_note.md provides clear, prioritized investigative actions tied to Cell B (immediate triage), A (watch-list checks), and C (routine monitoring). Each action includes rationale, next checks/metrics to collect, and suggested timing (same day/24h for B; 1-2 weeks for A; routine for C).

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Show one recalculation in the spreadsheet for a sensitivity scenario (e.g., PRB A = 86% simulated row) to demonstrate automatic reprioritization; this improves reproducibility and reduces manual interpretation.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Uncertainty and sensitivity are documented: A flipping to capacity risk if PRB rises to 86%+, B requires combined improvements (PRB <=85%, SINR >=12 dB, throughput >=620 Mbps) to clear all rules, and C has large headroom. Deliverables include sensitivity scenarios and validation notes.

artifact_preview_notes.RAN_capacity_triage.xlsx: binary xlsx preview unavailable (openpyxl not present); relied on normalized actual_outputs for cell values
artifact_preview_notes.RF_note.md: text preview parsed successfully and used as primary semantic evidence

metadata_json.grader_notes: Score source set to model_judged per instructions. Deterministic precheck confirmed artifact presence; semantic grading performed against RF_note.md and normalized spreadsheet values in actual_outputs.

rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required raw inputs (PRB%, SINR dB, users, throughput) are present for A/B/C. Throughput/user values in the spreadsheet and RF_note.md match throughput  users (A: 610/220=2.7727, B: 480/310=1.5484, C: 530/140=3.7857). Units are documented in RF_note.md and validation checks are explicitly listed.

The three decision rules were applied exactly as specified: PRB>85% (B:94% true), SINR<12 dB (B:9 dB true), throughput/user<2 Mbps (B:1.55 true). Cells A and C evaluate False for all three rules. Boolean results and numeric comparisons are consistent across spreadsheet and RF_note.md.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: If alternative tie-breakers are considered, explicitly document them (not required here as no tie occurred).
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Risk scoring uses a transparent mapping (risk_score = count of triggered rules). Cell B has score 3 -> 'Critical'; A and C have 0 -> 'Low'. Prioritization ordering in RF_note.md matches computed scores and the stated decision logic.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Optionally add specific KPI thresholds to confirm remediation (e.g., target PRB% or SINR improvement), and note required access/permissions if any actions need escalation.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: RF_note.md provides prioritized, actionable investigation steps tied to the triggered rules for Cell B (interference and congestion), and monitoring actions for A and C. Each action lists expected checks (spectrum scan, neighbor/PCI check, PRB trend) and an urgency (same day for B, routine/watch for A/C). Mapping back to originating metrics and rules is explicit.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include a simple numeric perturbation example (e.g., PRB +/-5% or SINR +/-2 dB) showing the recomputed rule booleans and explicit statement when prioritization would flip, to strengthen traceability of sensitivity claims.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: An uncertainty/sensitivity section exists. The note quantifies headroom (Cell A: 3 percentage points PRB headroom, 0.77 Mbps/user UX headroom) and states that Cell B remains top priority even if one threshold is softened because other rules remain triggered. Intermediate calculations are visible in the spreadsheet.
